title: Consumer Price Index (CPI), Goods & Services, Urban Areas — General & Major Groups (CBI, FY1375-1401 / 1996-2023)
alt_sources: iran-data-portal (1937-2014, already on wdi__FP.CPI) | sci-amar (2011-2020 by-group, already on iran_sci1399__cpi_by_coicop_group_1390_1399)
time_range: SH1375-1401 / 1996/97-2022/23 (27 fiscal years, continuous, across 6 base-year vintages)
notes: Transcribed directly from 6 editions of the Central Bank of Iran's own Annual Review PDF (already on disk at data/raw/cbi-iran/cbi-annual-review-wayback/, not re-downloaded): the 1379, 1384, 1389, 1394, 1399 and 1401 editions, each of which carries a 5-year rolling statistical-appendix table (Consumer Price Index of Goods and Services in Urban Areas) giving the General index plus Goods/Services special groups and ~12 major COICOP-style groups (food, housing, transport, health, education, etc). Picked a non-overlapping ladder of editions so the 27 years 1996/97-2022/23 are covered with zero gaps and only minor edition-to-edition overlap used for cross-checking (e.g. the 1384 and 1386 editions' figures for 2003/04-2005/06 match exactly, confirming transcription accuracy). Each vintage keeps its own stated base year (1997/98=100, 2004/05=100, 2011/12=100, 2016/17=100, or 2021/22=100) -- values are NEVER rescaled/spliced across base-year boundaries, per project convention; base_year_sh column lets a consumer re-derive year-over-year growth within each segment (pct_change_yoy column already does this arithmetic, computed from adjacent index values in the same vintage, and is marked 'computed' vs the handful of directly-source-stated values). CROSS-VALIDATION: computed YoY figures for 1391-1393 (30.5%, 34.8%, 15.5%) match the project's existing iran-data-portal-sourced inflation series (30.5%, 34.7%, 15.6%) almost exactly -- strong corroboration between two independently-obtained CBI-attributed series. ONE GENUINE DISCREPANCY FOUND, reported honestly rather than adjudicated: CBI's own 1384 Annual Review states 1384's (2005/06) inflation as 12.1% (both directly printed in the table's own 'percentage change' column AND reproducible from its adjacent index values, 307.6/274.5), whereas the existing iran-data-portal inflation_rate_1937_2014.csv gives 10.4% for the same year, both nominally CBI-sourced. Kept as two disagreeing values in two separate files rather than silently reconciled -- a future pass with access to the actual Iran Data Portal source page could investigate which vintage/revision it drew from. By-group detail is a genuine addition beyond what wdi__FP.CPI carries (which is only the rescaled 2010=100 general index) and beyond the existing iran_sci1399__cpi_by_coicop_group_1390_1399 chart (COICOP/SCI groups, 2011-2020 only) -- this file's groups are CBI's own (similar but not identical) classification and extend a further 15 years earlier (1996) plus 2 years later (2022) than the SCI file. WPI/PPI companion measures registered as separate rows below.

title: Producer Price Index (PPI) by Production Sector — General, Agriculture, Manufacturing, Services (CBI, FY1375-1401 / 1996-2023)
time_range: SH1375-1401 / 1996/97-2022/23 (27 fiscal years, continuous, across 6 base-year vintages)
notes: Same 6-edition ladder and extraction method as the CPI-Urban row above, same PDFs, same base-year-segmented / never-spliced discipline. PPI classifies output by PRODUCING SECTOR (agriculture/hunting/forestry/fishing, manufacturing+mining, services), a genuinely different axis from WPI's goods-flow classification (domestic/imported/exported -- see the WPI row below) and from CPI's household-consumption-basket classification. Registered as status=new because no existing chart_id represents a sectoral producer-price measure for Iran at all -- WDI's Price Indices category only has FP.CPI (consumer) and FP.WPI (wholesale, goods-flow); there is no World Bank 'producer price index' series for Iran to extend. One small internal inconsistency noted, not resolved: the 1399 and 1401 editions both report PPI for FY1399 (2020/21) but disagree slightly (331.6 vs 333.8, base 2016/17=100 in both) -- likely a routine preliminary-vs-final data revision between annual editions; both values kept, in their respective vintage rows, rather than picking one.

title: Wholesale Price Index (WPI), Goods-Flow Classification — General, Domestic/Imported/Exported (CBI, FY1375-1386 / 1996-2008)
time_range: SH1375-1386 / 1996/97-2007/08 (12 fiscal years) -- CBI itself discontinued this table after the 1386 edition; the 1387 edition's table-of-contents lists no WPI table at all, confirming the series ends here.
notes: This is the classic (Wholesale Price Index / shakhes-e beha-ye omde-forushi) the task brief specifically asked for -- distinct from PPI (see row above): WPI classifies goods by DOMESTIC/IMPORTED/EXPORTED flow, not by producing sector. Confirmed by direct inspection of the 1387 (2008/09) Annual Review's table-of-contents (only lists CPI + PPI, no WPI) that CBI dropped this specific table after the 1386 edition; PPI absorbed its role going forward. STRONG CROSS-VALIDATION: WDI's own FP.WPI.TOTL series for Iran (already in macro_wdi.csv) ALSO runs exactly 1960-2007 and stops in the same year -- near-certain confirmation that WDI's Iran WPI figure is itself sourced from this same CBI table lineage, not an independent World Bank construction. Registering as extends rather than new for that reason: same underlying measure, WDI just carries the single rescaled (2010=100) national figure with no goods-flow breakdown, while this file adds the domestic/imported/exported special-group split CBI itself published. Overlapping years across editions (1382-1384 appear in both the 1384 and 1386 tables) match to the decimal, confirming transcription accuracy.

title: Consumer Price Index, Urban vs Rural Households — General Index (SCI, SH1385-1399 / 2006-2020)
notes: Directly answers the task brief's explicit 'urban vs rural CPI' target measure -- genuinely new to this project; grep of CHART_REGISTRY.csv confirmed zero existing chart_id contains a rural CPI series (CBI's own Annual Review, mined separately in the 3 rows above, ONLY ever published an Urban CPI -- confirmed by full-text search across all 23 editions on disk, zero rural CPI hits). Source: SCI's own Iran Statistical Yearbook 1399, Chapter 22 (Price Indices), already downloaded at data/raw/sci-amar/sci-cpi-yearbook/sci_yearbook_1399_ch22_price-indices.pdf but only its Table 22.2 (national by-group breakdown) had been mined by a prior agent into iran_sci1399__cpi_by_coicop_group_1390_1399 (already registered) -- this row is the first extraction of that same PDF's Table 22.4 (Urban) and Table 22.7 (Rural), General-index column, base year 1395=100. Genuinely sparse (only 7 year points, not annual) because the yearbook table itself is designed to show a few historical benchmark years plus the 5 most recent years, rather than a full run -- left as-is, not interpolated. The PDF's own introduction (transcribed into this dataset's companion README) gives real institutional history: SCI's rural CPI survey dates to 1353 SH (1974/75), predating this table's earliest usable point by over a decade; a future agent could mine SCI's older (pre-1399) yearbook editions, if downloaded, for that deeper rural history. Rural inflation runs consistently higher than urban in every year shown (e.g. 1399: rural 37.7% vs urban 36.2% YoY) -- a real, citable finding, not a transcription artifact (both numbers independently verified against the source PDF's own printed percentage-change column).

title: Consumer Price Index, National/Urban/Rural — Monthly Point-in-Time Snapshots (SCI, 2022-2025)
time_range: 5 non-contiguous months: Esfand 1400, Farvardin 1401, Bahman 1401, Mehr 1402, Bahman 1403 (approx. Mar 2022 - Feb 2025)
notes: Bridges the gap immediately after the newest CBI Annual Review PDF on disk (1401 SH / 2022-23) forward toward the present. amar.org.ir (SCI's own domain) and cbi.ir's live inflation dashboard are BOTH unreachable from this sandbox -- amar.org.ir fails outright on direct curl (no HTTP response) and on WebFetch (ECONNRESET/'Socket is closed' on every URL tried, including its homepage); cbi.ir/Inflation/Inflation_en.aspx is reachable but sits behind an Incapsula JS bot-challenge, confirmed by fetching its OWN most recent Wayback snapshot (2026-05-30) and finding the challenge page itself was what got archived, not real data. Worked around by querying the Wayback Machine's CDX API for archived amar.org.ir monthly CPI press-release pages and fetching 5 of them directly via web.archive.org (which IS reachable). Genuinely sparse and NOT a substitute for a continuous annual series -- flagged honestly as such in the file's own notes column and in this project's logs/agents/iran-prices-inflation.progress.md. Still useful: base year (1400 SH = 2021/22 = 100) matches CBI's own 1401 Annual Review base year exactly, so the two data sources are directly comparable without rebasing. Also carries national/urban/rural point-to-point (YoY) inflation-rate splits for 3 of the 5 months, corroborating the rural>urban gap already documented in the SCI-yearbook-sourced row above (e.g. Bahman 1401: urban 52.7% vs rural 56.7%).
